"},{"inputs":[],"name":"messageTransmitter","outputs":[{"internalType":"address","name":"","type":"address"}],"stateMutability":"view","type":"function"},{"inputs":[{"internalType":"bytes32","name":"","type":"bytes32"}],"name":"pendingReleases","outputs":[{"internalType":"address","name":"recipient","type":"address"},{"internalType":"uint256","name":"amount","type":"uint256"},{"internalType":"uint256","name":"unlockTime","type":"uint256"},{"internalType":"bool","name":"claimed","type":"bool"}],"stateMutability":"view","type":"function"},{"inputs":[{"internalType":"bytes","name":"message","type":"bytes"},{"internalType":"bytes","name":"attestation","type":"bytes"},{"internalType":"address","name":"finalRecipient","type":"address"},{"internalType":"uint256","name":"unlockTimestamp","type":"uint256"}],"name":"relay","outputs":[{"internalType":"bytes32","name":"releaseId","type":"bytes32"}],"stateMutability":"nonpayable","type":"function"},{"inputs":[],"name":"usdc","outputs":[{"internalType":"address","name":"","type":"address"}],"stateMutability":"view","type":"function"}],"devdoc":{"kind":"dev","methods":{"claim(bytes32)":{"params":{"releaseId":"The The actual received amount (post-fee) is what gets locked, not the burn amount.","params":{"attestation":"Attestation bytes (from Circle IRIS attestation API).","finalRecipient":"Address that can claim USDC after unlockTimestamp.","message":"CCTP message bytes (from Circle IRIS attestation API).","unlockTimestamp":"Unix timestamp after which claim() is permitted."},"returns":{"releaseId":"     Unique ID \u2014 pass this to claim(). Also emitted in ReleaseScheduled."}}},"title":"TimeLockHook (v6 \u2014 self-relay design)","version":1},"userdoc":{"kind":"user","methods":{"claim(bytes32)":{"notice":"Claim time-locked USDC after the unlock timestamp."},"messageTransmitter()":{"notice":"MessageTransmitterV2 on this chain \u2014 called internally by relay()."},"relay(bytes,bytes,address,uint256)":{"notice":"Relay a CCTP v2 message and register a time-locked USDC release."}},"notice":"CCTP v2 time-lock contract deployed on destination chains (e.g. Ethereum Sepolia). \u250c\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2510 \u2502  FLOW                                                                       \u2502 \u2502                                                                             \u2502 \u2502  1. Sender on Arc calls CrosschainEscrow.initiateConditionalTransfer with:  \u2502 \u2502       recipient  = address(TimeLockHook)  \u2190 this contract on dest chain    \u2502 \u2502       hookData   = abi.encode(finalRecipient, unlockTimestamp, amount)      \u2502 \u2502                                                                             \u2502 \u2502  2. Circle attests the burn message.                                        \u2502 \u2502                                                                             \u2502 \u2502  3. Anyone calls THIS contract's relay():                                   \u2502 \u2502       TimeLockHook.relay(message, attestation, finalRecipient, unlockTs)    \u2502 \u2502     \u2192 internally calls MessageTransmitterV2.receiveMessage()               \u2502 \u2502     \u2192 USDC minted to address(this) [since mintRecipient = TimeLockHook]    \u2502 \u2502     \u2192 PendingRelease stored with a unique nonce-based releaseId             \u2502 \u2502     \u2192 ReleaseScheduled(releaseId, ...) emitted                             \u2502 \u2502                                                                             \u2502 \u2502  4. After unlockTimestamp, finalRecipient calls:                            \u2502 \u2502       TimeLockHook.claim(releaseId)                                         \u2502 \u2502     \u2192 USDC transferred to finalRecipient                                   \u2502 \u2514\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2518 KEY DESIGN INSIGHT (v6 vs previous versions):   Circle's CCTP v2 hook mechanism does NOT automatically call handleReceiveMessage   on the mintRecipient. USDC is simply ERC-20 transferred to mintRecipient, and hooks   only run if someone calls a Circle CCTPHookWrapper.relay(). We avoid that complexity   by making TimeLockHook itself the relay entrypoint: it calls receiveMessage() and   registers the pending release atomically. MessageTransmitterV2 is deployed via CREATE2 at the same address on all CCTP v2 chains:   0xE737e5cEBEEBa77EFE34D4aa090756590b1CE275 releaseId is deterministic per relay() call:   keccak256(abi.encode(block.chainid, address(this), relayNonce++))   \u2014 unique per chain, per contract, per relay invocation.   Frontend reads it from the ReleaseScheduled event emitted by relay().","version":1}},"settings":{"compilationTarget":{"src/TimeLockHook.sol":"TimeLockHook"},"evmVersion":"paris","libraries":{},"metadata":{"bytecodeHash":"ipfs"},"optimizer":{"enabled":true,"runs":200},"remappings":[":forge-std/=lib/forge-std/src/"],"viaIR":true},"sources":{"src/IERC20.sol":{"keccak256":"0x9ac7895c65853fb5b98f95d1f967e313f4a2a823a9a70409d73b48f23f1c7aa8","license":"MIT","urls":["bzz-raw://a24ccfc240c86caacbfe65266375f036381985555d8176b98cad4a9f98d23b5e","dweb:/ipfs/Qmf2WnEs4CVK9pmgQ4SAhtkZtg6qKhViY7G5gteJfJeeyy"]},"src/TimeLockHook.sol":{"keccak256":"0x23dcb8f88ad7bd778e2cb08d2cfadb0658251b6a3a0e7cb27f44feebc78873b7","license":"MIT","urls":["bzz-raw://8eaf8a1d6f7e26aba8aafb8292c68385e3175f564bc051acedb2dd9d2eaa039a","dweb:/ipfs/QmdQ1ojDDnuoVbnipSkwkhzGFepBu7ncpTzhjSco5wG7Cv"]}},"version":1}

params.releaseId: The ID emitted in the ReleaseScheduled event during relay().

relay(bytes,bytes,address,uint256).details: mintRecipient in the CCTP burn message MUST equal address(this).      USDC is minted directly to this contract by MessageTransmitterV2.receiveMessage().      The actual received amount (post-fee) is what gets locked, not the burn amount.

params.attestation: Attestation bytes (from Circle IRIS attestation API).
params.finalRecipient: Address that can claim USDC after unlockTimestamp.
params.message: CCTP message bytes (from Circle IRIS attestation API).
params.unlockTimestamp: Unix timestamp after which claim() is permitted.

returns.releaseId: Unique ID — pass this to claim(). Also emitted in ReleaseScheduled.

claim(bytes32).notice: Claim time-locked USDC after the unlock timestamp.

messageTransmitter().notice: MessageTransmitterV2 on this chain — called internally by relay().

relay(bytes,bytes,address,uint256).notice: Relay a CCTP v2 message and register a time-locked USDC release.
